desires: Finish the reading response draft before midnight so I can claim full academic credit without risking a grade drop.
Get this room cleaned up by tomorrow night to avoid having my study space compromised, but only if it doesn't delay my work further.
Convince Roommate_R that their presence isn't an obstacle to my success, or demand they step aside until my deadline passes.

goals.description: Execute a hard mental reset, relocate to the library desk immediately, and draft the reading response until submission.
goals.rationale: My value scale weights academic outcomes at 0.5; missing this deadline means losing the 'A' and wasting time explaining my absence later. I have 2 hours of sleep debt and can't afford to drag my feet. Relocating instantly clears the sensory bleed from the laundry pile so I can grind without panic. Social friction is secondary to hitting this target.

goals.description: Clean only my specific section of the room (target: zero odor in my zone) by 23:00 tomorrow to avoid the reputation cost of feeling like a failure.
goals.rationale: Health and physical comfort are baselines, but not worth sacrificing for perfection. I'm doing the bare minimum required to stop the smell from dragging down my focus during the actual writing session. Once the draft is submitted, I drop all social obligations regarding this topic entirely because negotiation drains energy I need for growth.

goals.description: Send one direct text to Roommate_R stating grades are priority #1 and their presence in the room must be paused until tomorrow morning; then shut down all communication channels regarding this topic.
goals.rationale: I'm tired of debating and wasting energy on it. My sleep debt is real, and I cannot afford social friction right now. However, I will assert my boundary once via text if absolutely necessary, but given the soft hardness of the cleanliness task and the high priority of the assignment, I'll defer this interaction to preserve cognitive bandwidth for the actual work.
